body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-42404", "cvss": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-918", "name": "Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)"}], "description": "Apache Neethi does not impose any restrictions on URIs when manually fetching remote policy references through the PolicyReference API. When an application explicitly calls the API to retrieve a policy from a remote URI, an outbound request is made for arbitrary protocols and internal IP adddresses. From 3.2.2, only http or https URIs are allowed, and link-local/multicast/any-local addresses are forbidden.\n\nUsers are recommended to upgrade to version 3.2.2, which fixes this issue.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00497, "percentile": 0.38632}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-287c-fxr7-3w6c", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-07T02:54:54Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-287c-fxr7-3w6c", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-287c-fxr7-3w6c"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-42404"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-01T11:16:19Z", "published_at": "2026-05-01T12:30:24Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-42404", "https://lists.apache.org/thread/zdspnt64zznyjyn648553kptx69w23oq", "http://www.openwall.com/lists/oss-security/2026/05/01/8", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-287c-fxr7-3w6c"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/apache/ws-neethi", "summary": "Apache Neethi doesn't impose any restrictions on URIs when manually fetching remote policy references through the PolicyReference API", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-07T02:54:55Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-287c-fxr7-3w6c", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "3.2.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "org.apache.neethi:neethi"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 3.2.2"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Apache Neethi doesn't impose any restrictions on URIs when manually fetching remote policy references through the PolicyReference API
